design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through themed Wild West stages, defeat boss outlaw, claim bounty reward. Cooperative shooting with risk/reward for damage contribution to bosses.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing single-hit lives while navigating enemy-filled stages and boss encounters; competitive damage contribution in multiplayer for boss bonuses.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a legendary bounty hunter in the American Old West, competing to claim the highest rewards by eliminating the most wanted criminals.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving single-hit-death lives system paired with cooperative multiplayer creates tension between cooperation and competition (damage bonus to highest contributor). Four distinct bounty hunters with unique weapons (revolvers, rifle, shotgun) encode different playstyles. Bonus minigames reward precision shooting rather than survival.

difficulty.lives_system: Single life lost per enemy hit, trampling, fire, explosion, or obstacle contact. Power-ups also lost on hit. Limited lives per credit with continues available.
difficulty.continues_system: After all lives lost, player may continue by inserting credits with limited time window before game over.
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within stages. Stage progression resets on continue.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High via hidden menu code: HARD mode limited to 3 extra lives and 3 continues (default), NORMAL allows 4 each, unlockable code allows up to 5 REST and 5 CONTINUE for any difficulty.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups: gold sheriff's badge (fully automatic fire), silver badge (second gun). Both equippable simultaneously. Dynamite (thrown), environmental weapons (rocks/barrels/torches), mounted Gatling guns in final stage.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight mandatory stages with wanted poster introduction for each boss. After stage 8 completion, game restarts with increased difficulty (loop). Bonus minigames unlock after stages 2 and 5.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct character playstyles with different weapons
Difficulty loop with escalating challenge after completion
Bonus minigame stages for score accumulation
Horseback and train stages for environmental variety
Cooperative multiplayer with competitive damage contribution mechanic
Easter egg: Cormano wears sombrero after defeating stage 5 boss

world_building_techniques: Wanted posters displayed at stage start naming specific outlaws
Stage themes establish Wild West setting (saloons, ranches, trains, horseback sequences)
Character-specific weapon design reinforces bounty hunter archetypes
Boss dialogue via text bubbles (SNES version) or voice acting (arcade)
Conservative costume design for supporting NPCs (barfly, dancers) adjusted in SNES port

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in single mandatory boss fight against a named outlaw. Boss victory grants bounty reward; multiplayer damage contribution determines who receives full bonus. Bosses serve as narrative payoff to wanted poster setup.

faction_indicators: Four distinct bounty hunters with different weapon classes (Steve/Billy: revolvers; Bob: rifle; Cormano: shotgun)
Named outlaws as antagonistic faction (Simon Greedwell, Paco Loco, Chief Scalpem, Sir Richard Rose, etc.)
Bounty reward economy drives player motivation
danger_signals: Single-hit-death mechanic creates high player vulnerability
Sack-carrying bandits as specific threat type
Female bandits carrying active dynamite (time-pressure threat)
Environmental hazards: fire, explosions, falling rocks/barrels/torches
Boss characters as escalating named threats
environmental_storytelling: Stage progression through themed locations: saloons, ranches, trains, horseback trails
Doorways containing hidden power-ups encourage environmental exploration
Mounted Gatling guns in final stage indicate militarized final confrontation
Saloon dancers and barfly NPCs populate frontier settlements

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Sunset Riders (SNES)
